statement: A single intrinsic conductance acts as the dominant pacemaker of cycle period: varying it shifts the pyloric cycle length monotonically over a wide range, while the triphasic phase pattern is preserved.
formal: There exists one intrinsic conductance g* such that a single-parameter sweep of g* produces a large, monotonic change in cycle period (large period-distance) but only a small change in the period-invariant phase pattern (small phase-distance). For non-pacemaker conductances, period-distance under the same sweep is comparatively small.
prediction: Sweeping the pacemaker conductance moves the period strongly (high period-distance) while keeping the phase pattern intact (low phase-distance); most other conductances move the period much less.
